description: Tree-structured AML motion-pattern matcher. Nodes follow a WordNet-like hypernym path: broad motion pattern parents first, lexical/activity aliases only as metadata. Runtime matchers consume Layer-3-derived evidence fields and never query WordNet.

nodes.label: ground, prone, kneel, and climb pattern

rationale.value: dominant root translation summarized as a gait-level family
salient vertical impulse overlaps or neighbors a translating root event
salient vertical impulse without reliable root translation
salient vertical impulse without reliable root translation
repeated low-body posture dominates vertical motion, so keep this as a low-body repetition pattern instead of a jump family
repeated low-body posture dominates vertical motion, so keep this as a low-body repetition pattern instead of a jump family
rotation is the strongest whole-body event
repeated vertical cycles co-occur with repeated bimanual raise-spread, so keep the primary family as a bilateral rhythmic coordination pattern
repeated bimanual raise-spread with small turns and low gait bounce is a bilateral rhythmic gesture pattern, not a fixed dance label
upper-body and bimanual events dominate without reliable locomotion or a named object/action cue
one arm has repeated or held upper-body motion but no stable semantic action name
only static or subtle state evidence is available, so keep the family conservative
no stable coarse family matched the event signature
bimanual events are the most interpretable fallback family
